description: This figure presents geometrically necessary dislocation (GND) density distributions in a duplex lightweight steel (Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni) obtained from in-situ EBSD tensile testing. Subfigures (a1-d1) display GND maps of the austenitic phase (γ) at strains of 0%, 1%, 3%, and 6%, while (a2-d2) show corresponding GND distributions for the B2 phase at identical strain levels, with the color scale indicating ρ_GND ranging from 10^13 to 10^16 m^-2. Subfigures (e) and (f) present frequency distribution histograms with Gaussian fitting curves for austenite and B2 phases respectively, showing the evolution from initial densities of 5.36×10^13 m^-2 and 1.04×10^14 m^-2 to 2.64×10^14 m^-2 and 2.51×10^14 m^-2 at 6% strain. Subfigure (g) plots the average GND density versus strain, revealing that while the B2 phase initially exhibits higher dislocation density due to its smaller grain size, the austenite phase accumulates dislocations more rapidly and surpasses B2 at 6% strain. The marked regions G1-G4 highlight heterogeneous GND distributions among individual grains. These results demonstrate co-deformation of both phases and provide evidence for hetero-deformation induced (HDI) strengthening mechanisms through dislocation accumulation at phase boundaries.
